behaviorIndicators: isConnected shows false despite mock returning true
Mock console logs instead of real data flow
contextClues: Real-time updates expected but not functional

failureSymptoms: isConnected shows false despite mock returning true
Mock console logs instead of real data flow

preventionMethods: Implement graceful degradation patterns and mock detection
Implement HTTP polling fallback and data refresh mechanisms

contextClues: Context switching from real to mock implementation

patterns.name: Error Boundary Inadequacy for WebSocket Failures

behaviorIndicators: No recovery mechanism for WebSocket failures

failureSymptoms: No recovery mechanism for WebSocket failures

earlyWarningSignals: Generic error messages for WebSocket failures
preventionMethods: Implement specialized WebSocket error boundaries with recovery

behaviorIndicators: Demo data shown as real data
Budget alerts based on fake data
Users making decisions on incorrect information
contextClues: No clear indication of demo mode
User cannot distinguish real from mock data

failureSymptoms: Demo data shown as real data
Budget alerts based on fake data
Users making decisions on incorrect information

preventionMethods: Implement clear demo mode indicators and user education

recoveryStrategies: Implement HTTP polling fallback and demo data with clear indicators
Display honest connection status and alternative indicators
Local notification management with HTTP sync

steps: Before adding WebSocket dependency: Write test for fallback first
During implementation: Ensure mock detection works
Before deployment: Verify fallback API endpoints
Post-deployment: Monitor fallback usage metrics

ciChecks: Test mock detection logic in CI
Check for console.log statements in production builds
Validate HTTP API fallback endpoints exist

steps: Before removing WebSocket: Implement and test HTTP fallback
During development: Test connection state transitions
Before deployment: Verify fallback API performance
Post-deployment: Monitor HTTP vs WebSocket usage

ciChecks: Test HTTP fallback endpoints exist and work
Verify polling stops when WebSocket connects
Test error handling in HTTP polling

steps: Before context changes: Test all consumer components
During development: Verify state transitions
Before deployment: Test mock/real mode switching
Post-deployment: Monitor context usage patterns

ciChecks: Test context provider with mock configuration

steps: Before adding WebSocket dependency: Add specialized error boundary
During development: Test error scenarios
Before deployment: Verify error recovery works
Post-deployment: Monitor error boundary activation

ciChecks: Test error boundary with different error types

steps: Before adding demo data: Test visibility of demo indicators
During development: Verify demo data is clearly marked
Before deployment: Test user can distinguish demo vs real
Post-deployment: Monitor user confusion reports

ciChecks: Check accessibility of demo mode indicators

trainingExamples.scenario: Component imports useWebSocketSingleton but WebSocket is mocked/removed
Component depends on real-time WebSocket events but has no HTTP fallback
WebSocket context provides mock data but child components expect real functionality
Error boundaries not configured to handle WebSocket dependency failures gracefully
Component loads demo data when WebSocket unavailable, confusing users about real vs mock data
